content:
- Started Choose-Task turn.
- Read prior progress, task summary skill instructions, active task summary, and done task summary.
- Next: refresh task summary, read choose-task rules, resolve blockers and precedence, then write the selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Refreshed .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md, read choose-task rules, and inspected all six active task files for blocker tags.
- Found no <blocked_by> tags on any active candidate. Active failing candidates are one bug, two smell sets, two story-29 tasks, and one story-30 task.
- Selected .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-image-amd64-has-quay-vulnerability-findings.md because the bug-first rule is mandatory and it is the only unblocked failing bug, with <priority>high</priority>.
- Next: execute that bug task with Red-Green TDD, then validate make check, make test, and make lint.

- Read prior Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-image-amd64-has-quay-vulnerability-findings.md.
- Next: inspect the task file contents and determine whether this turn is planning-only or execution.

- Read the bug task file and confirmed it does not yet contain a planning/execution marker.
- Determined this turn should create a plan artifact rather than modify production code.
- Next: inspect similar plan files and the verify image / publish / Quay-related code paths so the plan is concrete.

- Inspected the existing arm64 vulnerability-remediation plan and current verify image artifact harness/tests.
- Found that the repo already owns shipped-binary module-floor checks at the image artifact boundary, which is the correct code-boundary direction.
- Next: confirm the new amd64 advisory/module floor and then write a planning artifact plus task `<plan>` pointer for this amd64-specific follow-up.

- Determined the active amd64 verify-image vulnerability task was still in planning state because it had no `<plan>` pointer or execution marker.
- Added the task `<plan>` pointer and created .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-image-amd64-has-quay-vulnerability-findings_plans/2026-04-28-verify-image-amd64-vulnerability-remediation-plan.md.
- The plan keeps the artifact-boundary design from the completed arm64 fix, targets the stale pgx floor (`v5.9.0` -> `v5.9.2`), ends in `TO BE VERIFIED`, and says to quit immediately without implementation this turn.

- Started turn by reading previous progress, current task pointer, and required skill instructions.
- Intention is to determine whether the task file is still in planning state or marked NOW EXECUTE.
- Next step is to open the task file itself and follow the task-state instructions exactly.

- Opened the active bug task and searched for the plan pointer plus execution marker.
- Prior progress suggests a plan exists and still ends in TO BE VERIFIED, so this turn may need to stop after validating/planning state.
- Next step is to inspect the exact task and plan contents before making any edits.

- Confirmed the task file now has a plan pointer and the plan still ends in TO BE VERIFIED.
- Reading the plan plus relevant verify-image/quay/module-floor code so I can decide whether the design is ready for execution or needs another planning revision.
- Next step is to verify that the proposed pgx-floor change fits the existing artifact-boundary test harness cleanly.

- Inspected the existing verify image contract and artifact harness to validate whether the plan is executable as written.
- Also pulled the prior arm64 remediation plan to compare the intended code-boundary approach and avoid inventing a second mechanism.
- Next step is to decide whether the current amd64 plan is sound enough to flip to NOW EXECUTE.

- Re-read the active amd64 verify-image vulnerability task, its referenced plan, and the current verify-image artifact contract/harness.
- Re-verified that the existing artifact-boundary design is sound: the next turn should reuse VerifyImageArtifactHarness and tighten the pgx floor from v5.9.0 to v5.9.2.
- Updated .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-verify-image-amd64-has-quay-vulnerability-findings_plans/2026-04-28-verify-image-amd64-vulnerability-remediation-plan.md to add a short design re-verification note and switched the terminal marker from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE.
- Must quit immediately now. Next turn should start execution with TDD Slice 1 RED from the plan.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, and the required improve-code-boundaries and tdd skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is the amd64 verify-image vulnerability bug and prior turn switched its referenced plan to NOW EXECUTE.
- Next I am reading the task file plus referenced plan so I can execute the first red-green slice exactly as planned.

- Read the active bug task and its referenced plan; confirmed the plan ends in NOW EXECUTE.
- Executed TDD Slice 1 RED by tightening crates/runner/tests/verify_image_contract.rs from pgx floor v5.9.0 to v5.9.2.
- Proved the bug at the shipped-artifact boundary with `cargo test -p runner --test verify_image_contract verify_image_embeds_pgx_at_or_above_the_security_floor -- --ignored`, which failed because the built verify image embedded `github.com/jackc/pgx/v5` at `v5.9.0`.
- Next I need the GREEN step: raise the verify Go module dependency floor, refresh sums, and rerun the same focused contract before taking the next smallest failing slice.

- The first GREEN step upgraded pgx in go.mod/go.sum, but the verify-image build then failed in the Nix Go module packaging layer.
- Investigated the failure and confirmed the real stale boundary is the buildGoModule vendor hash in flake.nix, which still regenerates a vendor tree for the old dependency graph.
- Next I am refreshing the molt vendorHash values through a fake-hash build so the verify image can actually vendor pgx v5.9.2 and I can rerun the artifact contract.

- Tightened the verify-image pgx floor to v5.9.2, upgraded the Go dependency to v5.9.2, and traced the next failure to the stale buildGoModule vendorHash in flake.nix.
- Removed the mistaken local vendor attempt, refreshed both molt vendorHash entries to sha256-7yHLVPjLmZxUbe9MxCzK3jqIPWEV27XKFQl/0yDgt4o=, and reran the focused artifact contract.
- The focused public contract now passes: the built verify image embeds github.com/jackc/pgx/v5 at or above v5.9.2.
- Next I am running the smallest direct Go validation after the bump and then the full ignored verify-image contract suite to confirm no other artifact-level finding remains.

- Ran the full ignored verify-image artifact suite after the pgx/vendoring fixes; all 7 verify_image_contract tests passed, including pgx, grpc, x/crypto, entrypoint, runtime filesystem, and validate-config JSON logging.
- That re-verifies the original Quay bug at the correct public boundary: the built verify image now ships pgx at or above v5.9.2 and no other retained artifact-level floor test failed.
- Next I am running the required repository gates in order: make check, make lint, and make test.

- make test initially failed after the image fix because flake.nix still had the old vendorHash for verify-service-test.
- Updated the remaining verify-service-test vendorHash to the same refreshed sha256-7yHLVPjLmZxUbe9MxCzK3jqIPWEV27XKFQl/0yDgt4o= value and reran make test; it passed.
- Re-running make check and make lint against the final flake.nix state now, then I can mark the task complete and perform the Ralph switch/commit/push flow.
